zh.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwell是Cornwell的女兒，於大約1522年出生在英國英格蘭艾可布萊崔。她於1542年4月30日與Thomas Jacson在英國艾可特林結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1566年在她的家鄉享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可特林哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwell是Cornwell的女儿，于大约1522年出生在英国英格兰艾可布萊崔。她于1542年4月30日与Thomas Jacson在英国艾可特林结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1566年在她的家乡享年45岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可特林哥吉拉 沙智 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Cornwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, named from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

variant of Cornwall or Cornhill; in southeastern England mainly a habitational name from Cornhill in London but elsewhere alternatively from Cornhill (Northumberland) or one of the many minor places so called, often becoming Cornell .

variants.name: Cornwall
meanings: habitational name from the county of Cornwall, which is named with the Old English tribal name Cornwealas. This is from Kernow (the term that the Cornish used to refer to themselves, a word of uncertain etymology, perhaps connected with a Celtic element meaning ‘horn, headland’), + Old English wealas ‘strangers, foreigners’, the term used by the Anglo-Saxons for British-speaking people.

variants.name: Cornell
meanings: English: shortened form of Cornwell , Cornwall , or of Cornhill, a habitational name from a place in Northumberland named Cornhill, from Old English corn, a metathesized form of cron, cran ‘crane’ + halh ‘nook, recess’; or from Cornhill in London, a medieval grain exchange, named with Old English corn ‘corn, grain’ + hyll ‘hill’, or from some other place elsewhere similarly named.
English: possibly also a nickname from French corneille ‘rook, crow’, probably denoting a chatterer or someone with dark hair or a dark complexion.

variants.name: Corwell
meanings: English: possibly a habitational name from Cornwell in Oxfordshire, which in early medieval records is sometimes written without the -n-, for example Corwelle (see Cornwell ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwell was born about 1522, in Braintree, Essex, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Cornwell. She married Thomas Jacson on 30 April 1542, in Terling, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1566, in her hometown, at the age of 45, and was buried in All Saints Church, Terling, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1566, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterrée à All Saints Church, Terling, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1566, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolta a All Saints Church, Terling, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwellは約1522年にイギリスイギリスエセックスブレイントレでCornwellの娘として生まれた。1542年4月30日にイギリスエセックステルリングでThomas Jacsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1566年に故郷で45歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックステルリングアル サインツ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwell(은)는 대략 1522년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 브레이트리에서 Cornwell의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1542년 4월 30일 영국 에세스 털링에서 Thomas Jacson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1566년 그녀의 고향에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 털링 앨 세인츠 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Jacson em 30 de abril de 1542, em Terling, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Joan Cornwell родилась около 1522 г. в местности бреинтри, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали Cornwell. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Jacson 30 апреля 1542 г. в местности тирлинг, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1566 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 45 лет и была похоронена в местности алл сеинц чурч, тирлинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
